transcript: This is Bryce at United Parcel Service.
Y'all apparently ordered some, uh, bowling balls here. Chinese bowling balls. And, uh...
Um, I think you have the wrong number.
Th... there's... postage due and I need this... to pick up a check and drop them off.
I think you have the wrong number...
I didn't order any bowling balls from China.
No, there.... they didn't pay enough freight, so you... your balance is two hundred and thirty-eight dollars, ma'am.
Can you talk to my husband? [in background, unclear]
Hello? This is Bryce over at UPS.
Uh, apparently, y'all ordered some, uh... Chinese bowling balls...
...but there was no freight paid on it.

Well... hey... hey, wait a minute. Let me talk for a minute. We've never ordered anything from China, the last thing in the world would be bowling balls. So you know what you can do with those bowling balls?
Well, I... I won't tell you over the telephone.
How 'bout I come down there and tell you what you can do with 'em?
You can bring those buh... belding b... [sigh] ...bowling balls down here, and I'll have the police... wh... wh... ready for you when you get here. [unclear]
Sir, your balance is three hundred and eighty-two dollars. And they can--
I don't give a shit what it is, we didn't order them!
Do with them what you will!
Yeah, my name is Walter, I'm an administrator up here at UPS. What appears to be the problem here tonight, sir?

'[looped chuckle]' You have to be kidding. Now, look. Use your good common sense. We're standing here playing pool, and you think we ordered seven bowling balls from China? I told the other guy what he could do with the bowling balls.
